# UC-22.2.32 ¬∑ NIS2 Proportional Security Measure Verification by Tier

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Intermediate &middot; **Pillar:** Security &middot; **Type:** Compliance, Risk &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We compare the "who must have which safety alarm switched on" spreadsheet to what is actually running inside the security monitoring tool, tier by tier, so nobody accidentally lives with the stricter alarms turned off‚Äîor the lighter ones left on waste mode forever.*

---

## Description

Cross-checks the NIS2 control matrix against your active Enterprise Security correlation searches so you can prove required detective controls stay enabled for each entity tier‚Äîor expose intentional gaps with a ticket trail.

## Value

Helps proportionality reviews by translating "which tier needs which control" into live Splunk configuration state instead of slide-deck assertions, so internal audit can trace a disabled search back to a named `control_id`.

## Implementation

(1) Prefix every mandated correlation search with `nis2_` + `control_id`. (2) Version `nis2_control_matrix.csv` whenever risk committee moves a control between tiers. (3) Save `UC-22.2.32` weekly. (4) Auto-assign SNOW tasks using Splunkbase 1928 alert action. (5) Mirror outputs into GRC workbook quarterly.

## Evidence

`UC-22.2.32` CSV listing `control_id`, `entity_tier`, `search_disabled`, REST capture timestamp, and risk-committee minutes cross-reference stored in `audit_evidence`.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

Disable a non-production ES correlation search whose `title` matches `nis2_<control_id>` for a row where `required_for_tier=true`; the alert must emit that `control_id` with `search_disabled=1`.

### Negative scenario

Re-enable the same saved search (or delete the test object) and rerun within **15 minutes** so REST returns `disabled=0`, clearing the control from the failure roster.

## Detailed Implementation

### Prerequisites
- Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263) ‚â•7.0 deployed on a Search Head or Search Head Cluster. Confirm with `| rest /services/apps/local | search title=SplunkEnterpriseSecuritySuite | table title version`. The `SplunkEnterpriseSecurity` app context must exist ‚Äî the REST call in Step 2 explicitly scopes to this namespace.
- Service account `svc_es_readout` created in Splunk with capabilities `rest_properties_get` and `list_settings` but NOT `edit_saved_search` or `admin_all_objects`. This account reads ES correlation-search metadata but cannot modify or create searches. Configure via Settings > Access Controls > Users > Add User, assigning a custom role `nis2_es_rest_reader` with only `srchIndexesAllowed = _internal` (no data-index access needed ‚Äî this search uses REST and lookups only).
- CSV `nis2_control_matrix.csv` with columns: `control_id` (unique key matching the suffix of ES correlation-search titles, e.g., `INC-001`), `required_for_tier` (`true`/`false` per row), `entity_tier_applies` (`essential`, `important`, or `both`), `owner_team` (the team responsible for the control), `risk_treatment` (`mitigate`/`accept`/`avoid`/`transfer`), `last_reviewed` (ISO 8601 date). This CSV is the operator's NIS2 control obligations register ‚Äî each row represents a detective or preventive control that must be enforced for the indicated entity tier per the operator's Art.21(2) risk assessment.
- CSV `nis2_entity_classification.csv` (same as UC-22.2.31) providing the entity-tier context. Required because a control marked `required_for_tier=true` + `entity_tier_applies=essential` is only mandatory for entities classified as essential.
- Both CSVs stored in `etc/apps/SA-NIS2/lookups/` and distributed via Deployment Server to all SHC members. Version-control in Git with SHA256 manifest; changes require risk-committee approval and a ticket reference in the commit message.
- Naming convention: every ES correlation search that implements a mandated NIS2 control must be titled `nis2_<control_id>`, e.g., `nis2_INC-001`, `nis2_ACC-014`, `nis2_BCM-003`. The search in Step 2 uses this convention to map live ES configuration to matrix rows. Document this convention in the ES Content Management wiki and enforce it during correlation-search review.
- Network: the SH must be able to reach its own REST API on `8089/tcp` (`splunk_server=local`). In SHC deployments, the REST call runs on whichever SH executes the scheduled search ‚Äî the captain dispatches it. All SH members must have the `SplunkEnterpriseSecurity` app context (deployed by the SHC deployer); if one member is missing it, a search dispatched to that member returns 0 rows.
- License: this search consumes no indexed data ‚Äî it queries the REST API and lookups only. The only license cost is the summary-index output to `audit_evidence` (~1 KB per result row per run).

### Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
The "data" for this UC is not event data ‚Äî it is Splunk's own configuration state (correlation-search enabled/disabled) and the operator's control-obligation lookups.

**Control matrix onboarding**: export the control register from the GRC tool (ServiceNow GRC, Archer, OneTrust, or a spreadsheet). Map each control to an ES correlation search by naming convention. Validate the mapping:

```spl
| inputlookup nis2_control_matrix.csv
| join control_id type=left [
    | rest /servicesNS/-/SplunkEnterpriseSecurity/saved/searches count=0 splunk_server=local
    | search title="nis2_*"
    | eval control_id=replace(title, "nis2_", "")
    | table control_id title
]
| eval has_search=if(isnotnull(title), "yes", "MISSING")
| stats count by has_search
```

If `has_search=MISSING` count is > 0, those control_ids exist in the matrix but have no corresponding `nis2_*` correlation search in ES. Either create the search or document why the control is monitored outside ES (e.g., network-layer controls monitored by a SIEM-agnostic tool).

**Entity classification linkage**: confirm `nis2_entity_classification.csv` is current by cross-checking with UC-22.2.31 (the entity classification validation UC). If UC-22.2.31 is reporting mismatches, resolve those before this UC's output is meaningful ‚Äî a control marked `required_for_tier=true` for `essential` entities is irrelevant if the entity tier itself is wrong.

**ES correlation-search inventory**: run `| rest /servicesNS/-/SplunkEnterpriseSecurity/saved/searches count=0 splunk_server=local | search title="nis2_*" | table title disabled cron_schedule next_scheduled_time actions` to get the current state of all NIS2-tagged correlation searches. This is the baseline. Expect the count to match `| inputlookup nis2_control_matrix.csv | stats count`.

### Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Save as `UC-22.2.32 NIS2 Proportional Security Verification`.

```spl
| inputlookup nis2_control_matrix.csv
| join entity_tier [
    | inputlookup nis2_entity_classification.csv
    | stats values(entity_tier) as entity_tiers by legal_entity_id
    | mvexpand entity_tiers
    | rename entity_tiers as entity_tier
]
| where required_for_tier="true"
| join control_id [
    | rest /servicesNS/-/SplunkEnterpriseSecurity/saved/searches count=0 splunk_server=local
    | search title="nis2_*"
    | eval control_id=replace(title,"nis2_","")
    | stats latest(disabled) as search_disabled by control_id
]
| where search_disabled=1 OR isnull(search_disabled)
| table control_id, entity_tier, search_disabled
```

#### Understanding this SPL ‚Äî design decisions that survive review:

- **First join (entity_tier)**: the matrix has rows like `control_id=INC-001, required_for_tier=true, entity_tier_applies=essential`. The entity classification lookup has rows like `legal_entity_id=LE-001, entity_tier=essential`. The join on `entity_tier` connects the two: "this control is required for essential entities, and here are the essential entities." The `mvexpand` handles the edge case where a single entity has multiple tier classifications (rare but possible during reclassification transitions).

- **`required_for_tier="true"` filter**: drops controls that exist in the matrix but are optional for the given tier. Art.21(4) requires proportionality ‚Äî an important entity may legitimately skip controls that are mandatory only for essential entities. This filter enforces that distinction.

- **Second join (control_id to REST)**: maps each required control to its live ES correlation-search state. `latest(disabled)` gets the most recent configuration snapshot. `disabled=1` means the search is turned off in ES Content Management.

- **`isnull(search_disabled)`**: catches the case where the matrix requires a control but no corresponding `nis2_*` search exists in ES at all ‚Äî the control is not just disabled, it was never implemented. This is a more severe finding than a temporarily disabled search.

- **Why `| rest` instead of querying `_internal`**: the REST API returns the authoritative configuration state including searches that have never run. `_internal` only has entries for searches that have been scheduled and executed. A search that was created and immediately disabled has no `_internal` footprint ‚Äî REST is the only way to see it.

Schedule: cron `0 7 * * 1` (weekly, Monday at 07:00 ‚Äî aligns with the risk-committee weekly meeting). Time range: not applicable (REST is point-in-time, lookups are static). Trigger: `Number of results > 0`.

For daily monitoring (recommended during the first 90 days of NIS2 compliance), use cron `0 7 * * *`. Drop back to weekly once the mismatch rate is consistently 0 for 30 days.

Alert action: (1) Auto-create a ServiceNow task via `Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow` (Splunkbase 1928) alert action ‚Äî `assignment_group = nis2_risk_committee`, `urgency = 2`, `short_description = "NIS2 control [control_id] disabled or missing for [entity_tier] tier"`. (2) Email PDF to risk-committee distribution list. (3) Write results to `index=audit_evidence sourcetype=evidence:saved_search` for the 7-year evidence trail.

### Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
(a) **Positive test ‚Äî disabled search**: in a lab or non-production ES instance, navigate to Configure > Content > Content Management. Find a correlation search titled `nis2_TEST-CTRL`. Set Status = Disabled. Wait for the scheduled search to run (or trigger manually via `| savedsearch "UC-22.2.32 NIS2 Proportional Security Verification"`). Confirm a row appears with `control_id=TEST-CTRL, search_disabled=1`.

(b) **Positive test ‚Äî missing search**: add a row to `nis2_control_matrix.csv` with `control_id=NONEXISTENT, required_for_tier=true`. Run the search. Confirm a row appears with `control_id=NONEXISTENT, search_disabled=null` ‚Äî meaning the control is in the matrix but has no ES search at all.

(c) **Negative test ‚Äî enabled search**: re-enable `nis2_TEST-CTRL` in ES Content Management. Run the search. Confirm `TEST-CTRL` no longer appears in the output.

(d) **Proportionality test**: add a matrix row with `control_id=ESS-ONLY, required_for_tier=true, entity_tier_applies=essential`. Create the ES search `nis2_ESS-ONLY` and enable it. Confirm: the search does NOT fire for important entities (because `required_for_tier=true` only matched essential). This validates the proportionality logic.

(e) **REST API access verification**: log in as `svc_es_readout` and run `| rest /servicesNS/-/SplunkEnterpriseSecurity/saved/searches count=1 splunk_server=local | table title`. If this returns 0 rows or an error, the service account lacks the `SplunkEnterpriseSecurity` app context or the `rest_properties_get` capability. Check `index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd source=*splunkd_access.log* user=svc_es_readout status=403` for access denials.

(f) **SHC parity**: on a Search Head Cluster, the REST call should return the same results regardless of which SH member executes it ‚Äî because the deployer pushed the `SplunkEnterpriseSecurity` app to all members. Verify with `| rest /servicesNS/-/SplunkEnterpriseSecurity/saved/searches count=0 splunk_server=* | search title="nis2_*" | stats dc(splunk_server) as member_count values(splunk_server) as members by title | where member_count < 3` (replace 3 with your SHC member count). Any row means that search is missing from a SHC member ‚Äî typically caused by the deployer not having pushed the latest bundle.

### Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Dashboard (recommended name: `NIS2 Proportional Control Posture`):
- **Row 1 ‚Äî Headline tiles**: Single-value `Required controls disabled or missing` (red >= 1, green = 0). Single-value `% of required controls enabled` (target: 100%). Single-value `Last risk-committee review date` (from latest `nis2_control_matrix.csv` commit timestamp).
- **Row 2 ‚Äî Matrix heatmap**: rows = `control_id`, columns = `entity_tier` (`essential`, `important`). Cell color: green if search enabled, red if disabled, grey if not required. This gives the risk committee a single-page view of the entire proportionality posture.
- **Row 3 ‚Äî Disabled required controls table**: sortable by control_id, entity_tier, owner_team, risk_treatment. Drilldown opens the ES Content Management page for the correlation search.
- **Row 4 ‚Äî 90-day trend**: timechart from `index=audit_evidence sourcetype=evidence:saved_search search_name="UC-22.2.32*"` showing disabled-control count over time. Rising trend = configuration drift; flat zero = steady state.

Runbook (owner: NIS2 Risk Committee):
1. Monday 07:15: review the dashboard at the weekly risk standup.
2. If `search_disabled=1`: determine why the search was disabled. Common legitimate reasons: (a) scheduled maintenance bundle deployment that temporarily disables searches during rollout ‚Äî check `index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd component=DeploymentServer` for recent pushes. (b) Intentional seasonal pause (e.g., retail organizations pausing some fraud-detection rules during Black Friday to reduce false-positive load). Both require a time-bound exception in `nis2_exception_register.csv`.
3. If `search_disabled=null` (missing search): escalate to the SOC engineering lead. Either the control was never implemented in ES (gap) or the correlation search exists but was not named with the `nis2_*` convention (naming mismatch). Resolve by creating the search or renaming the existing one.
4. Quarterly: the risk committee reviews the full `nis2_control_matrix.csv` against the current risk assessment. Controls may move between tiers (e.g., a control previously required only for essential entities may be extended to important entities after a sector-level threat assessment). Update the CSV, push via Deployment Server, and verify the search output changes accordingly.
5. Export the dashboard as PDF, sign with RFC 3161 TSA, and archive to `index=audit_evidence` as the quarterly proportionality attestation artifact.

### Step 5 ‚Äî Troubleshooting

- **REST call returns 0 rows.** The service account `svc_es_readout` does not have read access to the `SplunkEnterpriseSecurity` app context. Fix: add the role `nis2_es_rest_reader` to `SplunkEnterpriseSecurity > Permissions > Roles` (Manage > App > Permissions). Verify with `| rest /servicesNS/svc_es_readout/SplunkEnterpriseSecurity/saved/searches count=1 splunk_server=local`. Check `index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd_access status=403 user=svc_es_readout uri="*/saved/searches*"` for 403 errors ‚Äî the `uri` field will show exactly which endpoint was denied.

- **REST call returns searches but `disabled` field is missing.** Older Splunk versions (<8.0) may not expose the `disabled` field in the REST response for saved searches that have never been toggled. Fix: explicitly add `| eval search_disabled=if(disabled="1" OR disabled=1, 1, 0)` to handle both string and integer representations. On Splunk 9.x this is consistent.

- **Matrix shows `required_for_tier=true` but the entity_tier join returns 0 rows.** The `entity_tier` values in `nis2_entity_classification.csv` do not match those in `nis2_control_matrix.csv`. Common mismatch: one CSV uses `Essential` (capitalized) while the other uses `essential` (lowercase). Fix: normalize both CSVs to lowercase, or add `| eval entity_tier=lower(entity_tier)` in the subsearches.

- **SHC member returns different result count than captain.** The `SplunkEnterpriseSecurity` app bundle was not pushed to all members after a recent correlation-search change. The deployer has the latest `savedsearches.conf` but one member is still serving stale configuration. Fix: on the deployer, run `splunk apply shcluster-bundle -target https://<captain>:8089 -auth admin:pass`. Verify with the SHC parity check in Step 3(f).

- **Alert fires every Sunday night, clears by Monday morning.** An ES maintenance job disables correlation searches during the weekly summary-indexing rebuild window. This is legitimate but should not trigger a compliance alert. Fix: check `index=_internal sourcetype=scheduler savedsearch="*nis2_*" status=disabled` for the timing pattern. Add a time-based filter to the search: `| where NOT (date_wday="sunday" AND date_hour >= 23)` or, better, suppress via `nis2_maintenance_windows.csv` keyed on `day_of_week` + `hour_range`.

- **`risk_treatment=accept` controls still appearing as findings.** The current SPL does not filter on risk_treatment ‚Äî all `required_for_tier=true` controls are surfaced regardless of whether the risk was formally accepted. This is intentional: accepted risk is still risk, and the risk committee should see it. If the committee wants to suppress accepted-risk controls, add `| where risk_treatment!="accept"` but document this decision in the risk register with a compensating-control reference.

- **REST API timeout on large ES deployments.** Environments with >500 correlation searches may see the REST call take >60 seconds and get killed by the search scheduler. Fix: add `count=0 timeout=120` to the `| rest` command, and increase the search's `max_time` in savedsearches.conf to 300 seconds. If the timeout persists, narrow the REST scope: `| rest /servicesNS/-/SplunkEnterpriseSecurity/saved/searches count=0 splunk_server=local search="action.notable=1"` to only return searches that create notables (correlation searches), skipping utility searches.

- **Control matrix CSV out of sync with GRC tool.** The Splunk CSV was last updated 6 months ago but the GRC tool has added 15 new controls. The search correctly reports those 15 as `search_disabled=null` ‚Äî but the finding is misleading because the ES searches may exist under non-`nis2_` names. Fix: run the mapping validation from Step 1 quarterly. Better: automate the GRC-to-CSV export via ServiceNow GRC's REST API (table `sn_grc_control`) and the Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow, so the CSV refreshes automatically.

## SPL

```spl
| inputlookup nis2_control_matrix.csv
| join entity_tier [
    | inputlookup nis2_entity_classification.csv
    | stats values(entity_tier) as entity_tiers by legal_entity_id
    | mvexpand entity_tiers
    | rename entity_tiers as entity_tier
]
| where required_for_tier="true"
| join control_id [
    | rest /servicesNS/-/SplunkEnterpriseSecurity/saved/searches count=0 splunk_server=local
    | search title="nis2_*"
    | eval control_id=replace(title,"nis2_","")
    | stats latest(disabled) as search_disabled by control_id
]
| where search_disabled=1 OR isnull(search_disabled)
| table control_id, entity_tier, search_disabled
```

## Visualization

Matrix heatmap of tier vs control coverage, table of disabled required controls, single value for percentage enabled.

## Known False Positives

‚Ä¢ **ES maintenance bundle disables searches temporarily** ‚Äî Distinguish: rollout window **30 minutes**. Suppress: join `_internal` `deployment` events or time-bound `nis2_exception_register.csv` with `change_ticket`.
‚Ä¢ **Testing copies of searches** ‚Äî Distinguish: titles include `_dev` suffix not in matrix. Suppress: `where match(title,"^nis2_[A-Z0-9]+$")` in rest subsearch.
‚Ä¢ **Renamed control IDs** ‚Äî Distinguish: matrix still references old `control_id`. Suppress: lookup `nis2_control_alias.csv` mapping old to new before REST join.
‚Ä¢ **SHC captain-only REST view** ‚Äî Distinguish: some members lack SplunkEnterpriseSecurity app context. Suppress: run saved search on captain with `splunk_server=local` service account role `ess_rest_reader`.
‚Ä¢ **Intentional seasonal pause** ‚Äî Distinguish: retail freezes correlation during peak sales with CISO approval. Suppress: lookup `nis2_control_pause_calendar.csv` keyed on week number.
